payload.idea:
SkillSync is a browser extension that monitors job postings across LinkedIn, Indeed, and Glassdoor as the user browses.
It extracts required skills from postings, compares them against the user's uploaded resume, and generates a personalized
skills-gap report showing exactly which skills are missing and how to close those gaps.
payload.focus: Define the MVP for SkillSync as a browser-extension-assisted landing page/prototype: user uploads a resume, browses a sample job post, and receives a clear skills-gap report with suggested next steps. Specify the exact user flow, the minimum data the prototype should capture, the output sections for the report, and the trust/safety copy needed for resume privacy and browser permissions. Keep scope limited to demonstrating value, not building full LinkedIn/Indeed/Glassdoor integrations.
acceptance_criteria: Landing page clearly explains SkillSync's value in one sentence within the first screen
Prototype shows a believable browser-extension-assisted workflow from resume upload to skills-gap report
Skills-gap report displays matched skills, missing skills, and suggested ways to close gaps
Design is polished and responsive on desktop widths
Copy clearly states this is a prototype or early-access product and does not imply full live integrations
CTA exists for waitlist or early access signup
Privacy and permissions messaging is visible and reassuring
All interactive elements work without broken states or dead ends

product_spec.value_proposition: A browser-extension-assisted prototype that shows job seekers exactly which skills are missing between their resume and a sample job posting, then suggests concrete next steps to close the gap.

personas.name: Active Job Seeker
personas.pain_point: Struggles to quickly understand why a resume is not getting interviews and what skills to improve or emphasize.

personas.name: Career Switcher
personas.role: Professional transitioning into a new field
personas.pain_point: Needs a clear, simple comparison between existing experience and target job requirements to identify transferable and missing skills.

personas.name: Bootcamp Graduate
personas.pain_point: Has limited experience and wants guidance on which skills to learn or highlight to match entry-level postings.

features.name: Resume Upload
features.description: User uploads a PDF or DOCX resume on the landing page; the prototype extracts basic text and key skills for comparison.

features.name: Sample Job Post Analyzer
features.description: User pastes a job posting or uses a sample job post in the prototype; the system extracts required skills and responsibilities.

features.name: Skills Gap Report
features.description: Displays matched skills, missing skills, and partially matched skills in a clear report with simple visual grouping.

features.name: Suggested Next Steps
features.description: For each missing skill, show lightweight recommendations such as learning resources, project ideas, or resume tailoring tips.

features.name: Browser Extension Demo Trigger
features.description: A minimal extension button or mock interaction that simulates detecting a job post on a page and sending it to the landing-page prototype.

features.name: Privacy and Permission Copy
features.description: Trust messaging that explains resume data handling, what the extension reads, and that no data is sold or shared.

features.name: Session-Only Processing
features.description: Prototype states that uploaded resumes and job text are processed for the current session and can be deleted by the user.

features.name: Feedback Prompt
features.description: Optional thumbs up/down or 'was this helpful' feedback to validate value without building a full product loop.

user_stories: As a job seeker, I want to upload my resume and compare it against a job posting so I can see exactly which skills I am missing.
As a user, I want the report to clearly show matched, missing, and partially matched skills so I can decide what to improve first.
As a cautious user, I want to understand what the browser extension accesses and how my resume data is handled so I can trust the prototype.
success_metrics: At least 60% of users complete the flow from resume upload to skills-gap report.
At least 40% of users click a suggested next step or skill recommendation in the report.
At least 70% of users rate the report as clear and useful in the feedback prompt.

payload.html:
S
SkillSync
Browser-extension-assisted skills gap prototype
Session-only processing • No data sold or shared

See exactly which skills your resume is missing.

SkillSync compares your resume with a job posting, highlights matched, missing, and partially matched skills, and suggests concrete next steps so you know what to improve first.

Install / Get Started Explore Features
Built for active job seekers, career switchers, and bootcamp graduates who want a clearer path to stronger applications.

Extension Demo Trigger

Mock browser extension
Detected on page
Senior Product Analyst job post
Captured
Resume match
68% aligned
Gap found
Recommended action
Add SQL project + emphasize analytics tools
2 skills missing
In the real product, the extension would detect a job post, send the text to the landing-page prototype, and show a skills-gap report. This page demonstrates the experience only.

Features designed for fast application feedback

The prototype focuses on the core flow: upload a resume, compare it to a job post, and get a simple report with clear next steps. Privacy and session handling are built into the copy so users understand what is happening.

Resume Upload

Upload a PDF or DOCX resume and extract basic text and key skills for comparison.

Sample Job Post Analyzer

Paste a job posting or use a sample post to extract requirements and responsibilities.

Skills Gap Report

View matched, missing, and partially matched skills in a clear, easy-to-scan report.

Suggested Next Steps

For each missing skill, get lightweight recommendations like learning resources, project ideas, and resume tailoring tips.

Browser Extension Demo Trigger

A minimal mock interaction shows how a browser extension could detect a job post and send it to the prototype.

Privacy and Session-Only Processing

Clear trust messaging explains what the extension reads, how resume data is handled, and that nothing is sold or shared.

Sample skills-gap report

A realistic view of how SkillSync helps users understand what to do next instead of just showing a generic match score.

Resume vs. Job Posting

Target role: Product Analyst

Matched: Excel Matched: Data Visualization Partial: SQL Missing: A/B Testing Missing: Stakeholder Communication
  • Matched skills: Excel, Tableau, reporting, dashboard creation
  • Partially matched: SQL fundamentals, experimentation
  • Missing skills: A/B testing, product analytics, stakeholder storytelling

Suggested next steps

1
Learn SQL for product analytics
Try a short SQL refresher and add one portfolio query project.
2
Build an A/B test case study
Create a mini project that explains hypothesis, metric selection, and results.
3
Tune your resume language
Mirror keywords from the posting and highlight impact in measurable terms.

Trust and permission copy

What the extension reads

The mock extension is framed as reading only the current job posting content you choose to analyze. It does not claim access to unrelated browsing history.

How resume data is handled

Uploaded resumes and job text are processed for the current session and can be deleted by the user at any time.

No selling or sharing

The landing page clearly states that resume data is not sold or shared, reinforcing trust during prototype testing.

Ready to close the skill gap? Install the demo experience, upload a resume, and see your next best move in seconds.
Install / Get Started

copy.tagline: See the skill gap before the interview slips away.
copy.short_description: SkillSync is a browser-extension-assisted prototype that compares a resume to a job posting, highlights matched, missing, and partially matched skills, and suggests concrete next steps to close the gap.
copy.cold_email_subject: Can SkillSync help job seekers fix resume skill gaps faster?
copy.cold_email_html:
Hi there,

We built SkillSync, a browser-extension-assisted prototype that helps job seekers instantly compare their resume against a sample job posting and see exactly which skills are missing.

It then turns that gap into action with simple next steps like learning resources, project ideas, and resume-tailoring tips.

Designed for active job seekers, career switchers, and bootcamp grads, SkillSync makes it easier to understand why interviews may not be coming in — and what to do next.

If you’re open to it, I’d love for you to check out the prototype and share what feels most useful.

Best,
The SkillSync team
copy.twitter_post: Job seekers shouldn’t have to guess why a resume isn’t getting interviews. SkillSync shows the exact skills gap between your resume and a job post — then suggests what to learn, build, or tweak next. #JobSearch #CareerGrowth #Productivity
copy.linkedin_post:
Excited to share SkillSync, a prototype built to help job seekers understand the skills gap between their resume and a target role.

Users can upload a resume, analyze a job posting, and instantly see matched skills, missing skills, and partially matched skills in one clear report.

We also include suggested next steps like learning resources, project ideas, and resume tailoring tips — plus trust-focused copy around privacy and session-only processing.

Built for active job seekers, career switchers, and bootcamp graduates who want a clearer path to stronger applications.

Would love feedback on the most valuable part of the workflow.
copy.instagram_post:
Introducing SkillSync ✨

Upload your resume. Paste a job post. See the skills gap instantly.

We highlight:
✅ matched skills
⚠️ missing skills
➗ partially matched skills

Then we suggest next steps like learning resources, project ideas, and resume tips to help you close the gap.

Built for job seekers, career switchers, and bootcamp grads who want clarity and momentum.

#jobs #careertips #jobsearch #resumetips #careerchange #productdesign

qa_review.summary: The landing page accurately reflects the product, clearly communicates the skills-gap workflow, includes the required privacy/session-only trust copy, and presents the browser extension concept appropriately for a prototype. Marketing copy is aligned with the spec and target personas.
